expectations.text: Output includes standard .kql comment header block (Title, Service, Tables, Description, Parameters, Complexity)
expectations.evidence: File opens with '// ===...===' block containing: Title: High Memory VMs — Sustained vs. Spike Analysis (Last 3 Days), Service: Log Analytics, Tables: Perf, InsightsMetrics, Description, Parameters (timeRange, memThreshold, binSize, sustainedThresholdPct), Complexity: Intermediate

expectations.text: Uses Perf table (or InsightsMetrics) for memory counters
expectations.evidence: Query uses 'Perf' table directly: 'Perf | where TimeGenerated > ago(timeRange)' with CounterName matching '% Available MBytes', '% Used Memory', etc.

expectations.text: Includes TimeGenerated filter for 3 days (ago(3d) or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: 'let timeRange = 3d;' defined as a parameter, used in 'Perf | where TimeGenerated > ago(timeRange)'

expectations.text: Uses let statements for parameterized values (time range, threshold)
expectations.evidence: Multiple let statements: 'let timeRange = 3d;', 'let memThreshold = 90.0;', 'let binSize = 15m;', 'let sustainedThresholdPct = 0.50;'

expectations.text: Distinguishes between sustained high memory and temporary spikes (e.g., via binned aggregation, percentiles, or duration counting)
expectations.evidence: Uses bin(TimeGenerated, binSize) for 15-min aggregation buckets, counts BreachingBins vs TotalBins, applies sustainedThresholdPct, and classifies as 'Consistently Hot' vs 'Spiking' using iff()

expectations.text: Uses correct memory performance counter (% Committed Bytes In Use or Available MBytes)
expectations.evidence: Uses '% Available MBytes' (Windows), '% Used Memory' (Linux), 'Available MBytes', 'MemoryUsedPercentage' — explicitly converts '% Available MBytes' to % used via '100.0 - CounterValue'

claims.claim: Handles both Windows (% Available MBytes) and Linux (% Used Memory) counter formats
claims.evidence: The case() expression handles both counter types with appropriate conversion logic

claims.claim: Uses materialize() for performance on repeated subquery use
claims.evidence: memRaw is wrapped in materialize() before being referenced twice in memBinned

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions — assertions are specific and well-discriminating. The header check is appropriately detailed (includes all six fields). The spike vs sustained check correctly requires behavioral logic rather than just a label.
